metadata.description: Code review for AI-assisted development — catches runtime failures that pass compile time, learns your preferences with Bayesian uncertainty, judges quality with a debiased LLM rubric.

plugins.name: full
plugins.description: Meta-plugin. One install pulls in all 7 Lich sub-plugins via dependency resolution.

plugins.name: lich-core
plugins.description: M1 Cousot Interval Propagation + M2 Falleri Structural Diff. The static-analysis substrate — flags division-by-zero, null deref, OOB, overflow via abstract interpretation; isolates semantic edits via GumTree AST diffing.

plugins.name: lich-sandbox
plugins.description: M5 Bounded Subprocess Dry-Run. Confirms M1's static flags by executing witness inputs in a stdlib-only sandbox (RLIMIT_CPU + RLIMIT_AS + signal.alarm). Unix-only at launch; Windows Phase 2.

plugins.name: lich-preference
plugins.description: M6 Bayesian Preference Accumulation. Per-(developer, rule) Beta-Binomial posteriors with Thompson sampling and a 5% surfacing floor. The Gauss Accumulation pattern.

plugins.name: lich-rubric
plugins.description: M7 Zheng Pairwise Rubric Judgment. 5-axis LLM-as-judge with position-swap debiasing and Cohen's Kappa reliability. Catches style/idiom/clarity signals static engines miss.

plugins.name: lich-python
plugins.description: Python language adapter. Maps ruff rule IDs into M-engine outputs; contributes Python-only idiom rules (pyupgrade, typing modernization, async-await).

plugins.name: lich-typescript
plugins.description: TypeScript language adapter. Maps biome rule IDs into M-engine outputs; contributes TS-only idiom rules (hooks exhaustive deps, JSX a11y, narrow-type-guards, any-avoidance).

plugins.name: lich-verdict
plugins.description: Cross-engine verdict synthesizer. Composes M1/M2/M5/M6/M7 outputs into DEPLOY/HOLD/FAIL verdicts and publishes lich.review.completed events.
